mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings declines, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and supply-side strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or the signal reverses direction during supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% imposes severe liquidity constraints on highly leveraged financial firms, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real GDP growth signal weakening aggregate demand and lower corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines show no negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.0% quarterly during post-recession recovery phases signals renewed manufacturing demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns specifically during recovery phases, or the signal performs worse than a buy-and-hold baseline in out-of-sample tests.
